title: Apple Q1 2026 Earnings Beat: Revenue $124B, EPS $2.40
text:
Apple Inc. (AAPL) reported fiscal Q1 2026 results that significantly beat Wall Street expectations on Thursday evening. The Cupertino-based technology giant reported revenue of $124.3 billion, up 8.7% year-over-year, surpassing analyst estimates of $121.5 billion. Earnings per share came in at $2.40, above the consensus estimate of $2.26.

The company's iPhone segment, its largest revenue driver, posted sales of $71.3 billion, representing a 6.2% increase compared to the same period last year. CEO Tim Cook highlighted strong demand in emerging markets, particularly India and Southeast Asia, as key growth drivers. Cook stated: 'Our iPhone business continues to show remarkable resilience, and we're seeing extraordinary customer response to our latest lineup.'

Services revenue hit a new record of $26.3 billion, growing 17.4% year-over-year, driven by the App Store, Apple Music, iCloud, and the rapidly expanding Apple TV+ subscriber base. The high-margin Services segment now represents 21.2% of total revenue.

Mac revenue came in at $9.4 billion, up 14.8% as the transition to Apple Silicon chips continues to pay dividends in performance and efficiency. iPad revenue was $8.1 billion, down 2.3% amid typical seasonal patterns. Wearables, Home and Accessories generated $12.0 billion.

The company's gross margin expanded to 46.9%, up from 45.9% in the year-ago period, reflecting the growing contribution of high-margin Services. Apple ended the quarter with $65.2 billion in cash and cash equivalents, net of debt.

Looking ahead, Apple provided Q2 2026 guidance of revenue between $90 billion and $93 billion, which was slightly above analyst expectations. The company also announced an increase to its share buyback authorization of $100 billion and raised its quarterly dividend by 5% to $0.26 per share.

Analysts reacted positively to the results. 'Apple has demonstrated once again that it can execute at the highest level,' wrote Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring, who maintained an Overweight rating with a $250 price target. 'The combination of strong iPhone demand and accelerating Services growth is exactly what investors were hoping for.'

Shares of AAPL rose approximately 3.2% in after-hours trading following the earnings release. The stock has gained 18.4% year-to-date through Thursday's close, outperforming the S&P 500's 6.1% gain over the same period.
